channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Why the NBA’s All-Star weekend has exploded since Oakland hosted event in 2000
item.description: The Warriors are hosting the NBA's signature winter event this weekend, just as they did a quarter century ago.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: City of Philadelphia asks Eagles fans to 'celebrate responsibly' at parade after slew of chaotic turns
item.description: After numerous arrests following the Eagles' last two wins, the city of Philadelphia is asking those in attendance at Friday's parade to "celebrate responsibly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: JuJu Watkins lifts sixth-ranked USC to program-defining win over No. 1 UCLA
item.description: JuJu Watkins broke out of a scoring slump with 38 points, helping sixth-ranked USC topple No. 1 UCLA 71-60 and further delivering on her goal of restoring the program to its earlier glory.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No. 1 UCLA's 23-game winning streak ends in 71-60 loss to USC and Watkins
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — JuJu Watkins scored 38 points to lead sixth-ranked Southern California over No. 1 UCLA 71-60 on Thursday night, handing the crosstown rival Bruins their first loss of the season.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: CMU Men's Wrestling "Battle in Brownson" recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) —Western Colorado Tops Colorado Mesa in ‘Battle in Brownson’ In a heated rivalry matchup inside Brownson Arena, the Colorado Mesa Mavericks faced off against the Western Colorado Mountaineers in the annual Battle in Brownson.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: McCarthy's big finish gives him the lead in the cold rain and brutal test of Torrey Pines
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Denny McCarthy ended a miserable day in a most brilliant manner Thursday at the Genesis Invitational, closing birdie-eagle for a 4-under 68 on a Torrey Pines course that more than lived up to its reputation as a beast.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Castroneves to use "world-class driver" provisional to race in Daytona 500 after qualifying crash
item.description: Four-time Indianapolis 500 winner Helio Castroneves will race in the Daytona 500 as a special 41st driver under a new rule that allows for a “world-class driver” to receive a provisional spot.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Fryer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jalen Hurts reveals why he wouldn't smile despite Eagles' massive lead in Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts was pouting and frowning on the sideline in new footage released by the NFL and explained why.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-UConn basketball player charged with murdering mother
item.description: Ex-UConn basketball player Eric Christopher Cobb was charged with murder after his mother was found dead and wrapped in a blanket in the backyard of her Florida home, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ten minute overtime? Longer TV timeouts? 4 Nations Face-Off is an NHL testing ground
item.description: MONTREAL (AP) — Canada and Sweden traded chances back and forth, skating up and down the ice for 3-on-3 overtime that provided dazzling entertainment in the opener of the 4 Nations Face-Off. By the time Mitch Marner scored the winner for Canada, more than six minutes had elapsed.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alex Bregman and Red Sox agree to $120 million, 3-year contract, AP source says
item.description: Alex Bregman and the Boston Red Sox have agreed to a $120 million, three-year contract, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Wednesday night. The person spoke to the AP on condition of anonymity because the agreement was subject to a successful physical.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cowboys great Emmitt Smith questions team's direction under Jerry Jones amid Brian Schottenheimer's promotion
item.description: The three-time Super Bowl champion and Cowboys icon pushed back against the idea that adding a star running back to the team would solve all of Dallas' issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White Sox sign veteran CF Michael A. Taylor to 1-year deal
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Center fielder Michael A. Taylor and the Chicago White Sox have agreed to a $1.95 million, one-year contract. Chicago announced the agreement Wednesday. Taylor spent last season with the Pittsburgh Pirates, batting .193 with five home runs, 21 RBI and a .543 OPS in 113 games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: From the same Minnesota hill that produced Lindsey Vonn, Paula Moltzan wins bronze at ski worlds
item.description: SAALBACH-HINTERGLEMM, Austria (AP) — Finishing fourth when medals are on the line can feel like cruel punishment. Paula Moltzan knows the feeling all too well. Already at this year's Alpine skiing world championships, she was on the U.S. squad that finished fourth in the mixed team parallel event.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eagles star Cooper DeJean reveals message Chiefs fan Caitlin Clark sent after Super Bowl victory
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles rookie cornerback Cooper DeJean said fellow Iowa grad Caitlin Clark sent him a congratulatory message after his Super Bowl win.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Padres sign Kyle Hart, left-hander with standout season in Korea
item.description: The San Diego Padres have agreed to a one-year contract with left-hander Kyle Hart out of Korea, according to an announcement by Major League Baseball Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Briscoe gives Toyota its first Daytona 500 pole
item.description: It is the first Daytona 500 pole for Briscoe, who moved to Joe Gibbs Racing this season when Stewart-Haas Racing closed at the end of 2024. His move to the No. 19 gave Toyota its first pole in NASCAR's biggest race of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Jed Hoyer reacts after Alex Bregman passes over Cubs, signs with Red Sox, according to reports
item.description: NOTE: In the attached video from spring training, Cubs President Jed Hoyer discusses missing out on a bid to sign All-Star third baseman Alex Bregman in free agency. Click to watch.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Oakland high school gym named after NBA legend, alumnus Bill Russell set to open
item.description: (KRON/BCN) -- The Oakland Unified School District will hold a ribbon-cutting ceremony on Wednesday for a new school gymnasium at McClymonds High School named after late NBA legend Bill Russell, who was an alumnus of the school. The ceremony will take place at 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: UPenn could face civil rights violation after trans athlete ban
item.description: It follows President Donald Trump's executive order banning transgender women from competing in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: Super Bowl returning to the Bay Area for 2026
item.description: (KRON) -- The Super Bowl is coming back to the Bay Area next year. A decade ago, the region hosted Super Bowl 50. Next year, it will play host to Super Bowl LX. On Monday morning, San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan was in New Orleans for the official Super Bowl handoff.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah HC earns first shootout victory over Capitals
item.description: WASHINGTON D.C. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Hockey Club is carrying a ton of momentum into the Four Nations Break. Karel Vejmelka made 30 saves in relief, and the Utah Hockey Club recovered for a 5-4 shootout win against the Washington Capitals at Capital One Arena on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Did you know? The flag presented during the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX came from Utah
item.description: The flag presented during the performance of the national anthem at Super Bowl LIX was handmade by a Utah-based flag company.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: A presidential first: Trump at the Super Bowl, latest chapter in a complicated legacy with football
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — As a student, Donald Trump played high school football. As a business baron, he owned a team in an upstart rival to the NFL and then sued the established league. As president, he denigrated pros who took a knee during the national anthem as part of a social justice movement.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Secret Service unveils Super Bowl ad
item.description: “America was founded on an idea of freedom. America's always stepped forward in time of need throughout our short but powerful history,” says a voiceover in the video.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: What's at stake, who's announcing, who's performing and what time does it start? Everything to know
item.description: Sean Lewis and Jewell Hillery talked with Rod Carter, live from New Orleans for The Big Game, on Sunday's WGN Weekend Morning News. See the attached video player to watch. Super Bowl Sunday weekend has arrived.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CMU Football signing day recap
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX)— The football program is entering a new era after 42 student-athletes officially committed to the Mavericks, officials said. “The thing I tell these guys when they go through recruiting is, ‘You’ve got to be where your feet are.
item.dc:creator: Titus Cleveland, KREX 5

item.title: White Sox agree to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago White Sox have agreed to a minor league contract with infielder Brandon Drury that includes an invitation to big league spring training camp. The team announced the move on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Devin Booker's 47 points leads Suns past Jazz in OT
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) - Devin Booker scored a season-high 47 points, Grayson Allen hit a 3-pointer at the buzzer to force overtime and the short-handed Phoenix Suns rallied to beat the Utah Jazz 135-127 on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Ryan Donato stars as the Blackhawks beat the slumping Predators 6-2
item.description: Ryan Donato had two goals and two assists, and the Chicago Blackhawks handed Nashville its sixth straight loss with a 6-2 victory over the Predators on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Shaun King and the Bucs’ Super Bowl XXXVII Victory: A Different View
item.description: Shaun King was at Qualcomm Stadium in San Diego, watching his team, the Tampa Bay Buccaneers, dominate Super Bowl XXXVII. It was surreal. Just a few seasons ago, he had been under center, leading this franchise through tough playoff battles.
item.dc:creator: Rod Carter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NFL has no plans to end DEI practices, commissioner says
item.description: The NFL is committed to sticking to its DEI policies despite a change in its Super Bowl on-field messaging campaign
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation
